name: samvaad-platform-facts
description: Sarvam Samvaad verified facts (2026-07-14): self-serve public since ~June 8 2026; dashboard.sarvam.ai/agents 308-redirects to platform.sarvam.ai/agents (login-gated, 403 to bots). Channels confirmed: telephone in+out, WhatsApp (incl voice calls), web/mobile SDKs (JS/RN/Flutter) per AWS Marketplace listing prodview-x5iks3edtmaio. Exotel is Sarvam's telephony backbone (exotel.com blog). No public Samvaad docs on docs.sarvam.ai (llms.txt has only LiveKit/Pipecat DIY guides, which inherit the public-API reasoning delay). Samvaad reportedly runs sarvam-30b privately. Self-serve per-minute pricing NOT public; API pricing: STT Rs30/hr, bulbul:v3 Rs30/10K chars, sarvam-30b Rs2.5/10 per 1M tokens in/out, Rs100 free API credits.

title: Two-track Samvaad migration, gated on 4 in-platform checks
rationale: Latency alone no longer forces migration: custom stack + fast-LLM bridge (already shipped, needs only CHAT_LLM_API_KEY) reaches ~1-1.5s, within ~0.5s of realistic Samvaad numbers (independent tests 0.8-1.8s vs sub-500ms marketing). Recommendation in docs/samvaad-migration-plan.md: Track 1 set fast-LLM key + re-export prod store now (Railway volume wedged); Track 2 pilot Marathi agent on platform.sarvam.ai, decide at gates G1 telephony path exists, G2 median latency <=1.5s measured, G3 cost <=2x current ~Rs2-3/min, G4 voice/transfer parity. WS-audio bridge into Samvaad rejected: no public media-ingress API.

name: marathi-voice-landscape-2026-07
description: Landscape survey of realtime Marathi voice agents (docs/marathi-realtime-voice-agents.md, 2026-07-15). Key deltas since the migration plan: (1) Sarvam shipped streaming STT WebSocket (saaras:v3 default, saarika:v2.5 now legacy, <150ms TTFT claimed) — drop-in upgrade for src/sarvam/stt.ts that pays off in every branch; (2) sarvam-105b released, ~2.1s TTFT at high reasoning per Artificial Analysis — probe with reasoning_effort=low before assuming it can't replace the fast-LLM bridge; (3) ranked Samvaad fallbacks: ElevenLabs Agents (11 Indic langs, documented SIP-trunk ingress, ~/bin/zsh.08/min + LLM + telco) if G1/G4 fail; Gemini Live native-audio S2S via Pipecat (~₹1.3-2.2/min, Marathi listed, deletes VAD/barge-in/STT/TTS code) as long-term DIY end-state; (4) Deepgram/Vapi/Retell rejected — no Marathi depth; (5) outbound on any BYO-telephony path needs TRAI DLT registration first.

name: prod-railway-down-2026-07-15
description: 2026-07-15: prod https://calling-agent-production-d954.up.railway.app returns Railway edge fallback 'Application not found' (header x-railway-fallback:true, JSON code 404) on ALL paths incl /healthz and /api/admin/export. This is 'no deployment mapped to this domain', NOT a cold-start or 401 — the Railway service is not running. Consequence: Track 1 export (/api/admin/export, auth via 'Authorization: Bearer <DASHBOARD_PASSWORD>') is unreachable until a human restores the service in Railway. The genuinely current live agent config (मराठी सहाय्यक, bulbul:v3/priya, DID number mapping per plan §4) lives only on the wedged Railway volume; local data/db.json is a stale Jul-8 snapshot (bulbul:v2/anushka, no numbers). Bridge code needs no changes.

name: railway-two-service-cards-2026-07-15
description: 2026-07-15: the Railway project (b11d25b9) now shows TWO service cards: original 'calling-agent' (id 58eec936) and a newer 'calling-agent-d1f7d584-1ff7-4aeb-9699-f852f12480d3' (id d1f7d584-1ff7-4aeb-9699-f852f12480d3). This is the likely cause of the prod 'Application not found' (see prod-railway-down-2026-07-15): the public domain calling-agent-production-d954.up.railway.app is bound to a card with no live deployment while the other card holds/should-hold the app. Resolution: in Railway, identify which card has a healthy deployment + volume mounted at /data, and bind the domain to THAT card (Settings > Networking/Domains). Both steps of Track 1 (seeds voice calling-c0b8) stay blocked until prod responds 200 on /healthz.

name: track1-resolved-v2-live-2026-07-15
description: RESOLUTION of prod-railway-down alarm (supersedes the 'config maybe lost' worry): the app was migrated to a NEW Railway service 'calling-agent-v2' (id 4a79287c-1bdb-4d97-83a0-4df6cc3d194b, project b11d25b9, env production 4734b6a2), deployed 2026-07-15 13:38Z, HEALTHY at https://calling-agent-v2-production.up.railway.app (/healthz=200, sarvamConfigured=true, receiving VoiceLink webhooks/streams as of 14:07Z). Its fresh volume 'calling-agent-v2-volume' (/data, READY) holds the CURRENT store: agent मराठी सहाय्यक mr-IN bulbul:v3/priya, number +919429391375 (agent 93a57c02-58ae-4b2d-a3c3-f56033832bbb, label 'fin'), 34 calls. TRACK 1 STEP 2 ALREADY DONE: CHAT_LLM_BASE_URL (=https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/openai, len55), CHAT_LLM_API_KEY (len53), CHAT_LLM_MODEL (=gemini-2.5-flash, len16) all set on v2 -> bridge active. TRACK 1 STEP 1: export reachable at v2 /api/admin/export (Bearer DASHBOARD_PASSWORD); still worth saving a durable off-box copy. The OLD service (58eec936, renamed calling-agent-d1f7d584-...) is DEAD (no service instance) and owned the now-404 vanity domain calling-agent-production-d954; VoiceLink already works against v2 so that URL is likely abandoned. Leftovers to clean (with user OK): dead old service + its 4.2MB volume calling-agent-volume-eJiO, and an ORPHANED 85MB volume calling-agent-volume (serviceId null, the ghost/wedged volume). Railway account token works via GraphQL/CLI (workspace 'hrubee's Projects' 27d53f78).

title: Dropped the pre-reply filler (हम्म.) on calling-agent-v2
rationale: Per migration-plan §4: the filler existed only to mask the old 5-10s sarvam-30b reasoning gap. With the Gemini flash-lite bridge live, LLM first-token is ~1s and total first-audio ~2s, so the filler just prepended an unnecessary 'हम्म.' before every reply. Set FILLER_ENABLED=false via Railway variableUpsert on service 4a79287c (env production 4734b6a2) + redeploy (bcf968ea). User confirmed the delay was gone and asked to remove the prefix. Reversible: set FILLER_ENABLED=true to restore.

title: Switched agent to neha voice + colloquial code-mixed Marathi
rationale: User: phone voice sounded artificial + wanted natural (not pure) Marathi with some English. Done via PUT /api/agents/93a57c02-58ae-4b2d-a3c3-f56033832bbb on calling-agent-v2 (no redeploy — agent config read per call). (1) ttsSpeaker priya->neha (bulbul:v3 kept, it's Sarvam's recommended TTS; priya is tagged IVR, neha is 'warm female, conversational'). Greeting cache keys on speaker (greeting.ts:14) so it auto-resynthesizes in neha next call. (2) systemPrompt rewritten (Marathi) to speak everyday spoken Marathi, freely code-mix common English loanwords BUT written in Devanagari (अकाउंट/ओके/पेमेंट) so mr-IN bulbul TTS pronounces them naturally, and avoid bookish/pure Marathi. Note: buildSystemPrompt (prompt.ts:10) still appends generic 'Always reply in mr-IN' but persona dominates; code-mix is still mr-IN so no conflict. Reversible via another PUT. bulbul:v3 has 38 speakers; warm-female alts: neha/pooja; energetic: tanya/suhani/niharika; male conversational: rahul/kabir.

title: Agent voice set to MALE rahul + colloquial code-mixed Marathi
rationale: UPDATE: user prefers a MALE voice. Current live voice on calling-agent-v2 agent 93a57c02 = bulbul:v3/rahul (professional male, conversational). Set via PUT /api/agents/:id (no redeploy; read per call; greeting auto-resynthesizes since greeting.ts cache keys on speaker). Prior interim pick was neha (female) — superseded. Code-mixed Marathi systemPrompt unchanged (everyday spoken Marathi + English loanwords in Devanagari, avoid pure/bookish Marathi). Male alternatives: kabir (warm), vijay/anand/gokul (mature/authoritative), aditya (news-anchor). bulbul:v3 stays the TTS model (Sarvam's best).

title: enable_preprocessing + pace 0.9 on both Sarvam TTS paths
rationale: Phone Marathi sounded less accurate than the Sarvam web demo; preprocessing normalizes numerals/mixed-script tokens before synthesis and pace 0.9 improves prosody clarity on the unavoidable 8kHz telephony floor

name: tts-quality-tuning-v2
description: 2026-07-15 TTS quality tuning live on calling-agent-v2 (agent 93a57c02). (A) TTS_STREAMING=false env -> REST full-text synthesis (whole reply at once, better prosody than per-sentence WS streaming; ~1s more first-audio). (B) code change (commit 081b2ae, merged 41a9471, deploy 98deec15): src/sarvam/tts.ts + ttsStream.ts now send enable_preprocessing:true (normalizes numbers/dates/code-mix for cleaner pronunciation) and REST pace default 1.0->0.9 for phone clarity. HARD CEILING remains: phone audio is 8kHz G.711 narrowband vs Sarvam web demo 24kHz — A+B are incremental; true demo fidelity needs a wideband channel (WhatsApp/app SDK), not PSTN. Voice=bulbul:v3/rahul. main is ahead of origin (coordinator cannot push; user must 'git push').
